steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first part of the sentence establishes a problem: artisanal cheese claims lack guaranteed veracity. The second part gives the response: the EU established DOP status. The transition word must show that the EU's action directly resulted from that problem — a cause-and-effect relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Conversely,: Signals contrast or opposition, not a result. The EU's action isn't opposite to the problem — it's a response to it. ✗
  • Choice B — Likewise,: Signals similarity or addition, not consequence. ✗
  • Choice C — Regardless,: Signals dismissal of the previous point, implying the EU acted despite the problem rather than because of it. ✗
  • Choice D — Therefore,: Directly signals that what follows is a consequence of what came before — establishing DOP status resulted from the need to guarantee veracity. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
